title: Login fails for users with special characters in password
body:
When a user has a password containing special characters like `!@#$%^&*`, the login endpoint returns a 500 error instead of authenticating the user. This affects approximately 15% of our user base based on our password policy stats.

Steps to reproduce:
1. Create an account with password `P@ssw0rd!23`
2. Log out
3. Attempt to log in — server returns 500

Expected: Successful authentication
Actual: 500 Internal Server Error

Server logs show: `psycopg2.errors.InvalidEscapeSequence` — looks like the password isn't being sanitized before the SQL query.
comments:
Confirmed on staging too. Looks like the bcrypt compare step is fine but there's a raw SQL fallback path that's not using parameterized queries. — dev@example.com

This is causing support tickets to pile up. Roughly 40 users impacted so far.
